name: Eco.Console

unions.name: Handle
unions.comment: A console handle identifying an output stream.

values.name: log
values.comment:
Debug-style trace function. Writes `tag` to stderr and returns `value`
unchanged. Mirrors `Debug.log` but is allowed under `--optimize` because
it is not a `Debug.*` function.

values.name: readAll
values.comment: Read all of stdin as a string.

values.name: readLine
values.comment: Read one line from stdin.

values.name: stderr
values.comment: Standard error handle.

values.name: stdout
values.comment: Standard output handle.

values.name: write
values.comment: Write a string to a console handle (stdout or stderr).

name: Eco.Crash

values.name: crash
values.comment: Crash the program with an error message. Never returns.

name: Eco.Env
comment:
Environment operations: look up env vars and CLI args.

All operations are atomic IO primitives backed by kernel implementations.


# Operations

@docs lookup, rawArgs

values.name: lookup
values.comment: Look up an environment variable by name. Returns Nothing if not set.

values.name: rawArgs
values.comment: Get the raw CLI arguments as a list of strings.

name: Eco.File

unions.name: Handle
unions.comment: An opaque file handle for reading, writing, or querying file metadata.

unions.name: IOMode
unions.comment: The mode in which a file is opened.

values.name: appDataDir
values.comment: Get the application-specific user data directory.

values.name: canonicalize
values.comment: Resolve symlinks and normalize a path.

values.name: close
values.comment: Close a file handle.

values.name: createDir
values.comment:
Create a directory. If the first argument is True, parent directories are
created as needed.

values.name: dirExists
values.comment: Check if a directory exists at the given path.

values.name: fileExists
values.comment: Check if a file exists at the given path.

values.name: findExecutable
values.comment: Search for an executable on the system PATH.

values.name: getCwd
values.comment: Get the current working directory.

values.name: hWriteString
values.comment: Write a string to a file handle.

values.name: list
values.comment: List the contents of a directory.

values.name: lock
values.comment: Acquire a lock on a file. Blocks until the lock is acquired.

values.name: modificationTime
values.comment: Get the modification time of a file.

values.name: open
values.comment: Open a file handle with the given mode.

values.name: readBytes
values.comment: Read a file as raw bytes.

values.name: readString
values.comment: Read a file as a UTF-8 string.

values.name: removeDir
values.comment: Remove a directory and all its contents recursively.

values.name: removeFile
values.comment: Remove a file.

values.name: setCwd
values.comment: Set the current working directory.

values.name: size
values.comment: Get the size of a file in bytes via its handle.

values.name: touch
values.comment:
Update the modification time of a file to the current time.
Creates the file if it does not exist.

values.name: unlock
values.comment: Release a lock on a file.

values.name: writeBytes
values.comment: Write raw bytes to a file.

values.name: writeString
values.comment: Write a UTF-8 string to a file.

name: Eco.Http
comment:
HTTP operations: fetch URLs and download archives.

All operations are atomic IO primitives backed by kernel implementations.


# Operations

@docs fetch, getArchive

values.name: fetch
values.comment:
Fetch a URL with the given HTTP method and headers.
Returns the response body on success, or an error record on failure.
values.type: String.String -> String.String -> List.List ( String.String, String.String ) -> Task.Task Basics.Never (Result.Result Eco.Http.Error.HttpError String.String)

values.name: getArchive
values.comment:
Download and extract a ZIP archive from a URL.
Returns a record with sha and archive entries on success, or an error message on failure.
values.type: String.String -> Task.Task Basics.Never (Result.Result String.String { sha : String.String, archive : List.List { relativePath : String.String, data : String.String } })

name: Eco.Http.Error

unions.name: HttpError
cases: { url : String.String, statusCode : Basics.Int, statusText : String.String }

values.name: decode
values.comment:
Refine the kernel's `( statusCode, statusText )` failure tuple into an
`HttpError`, given the request URL.

values.name: toString
values.comment: A short human-readable description, for embedding in larger messages.

name: Eco.IO.Error
comment:
Structured IO error types shared by Eco.File, Eco.Console, parts of
Eco.Process, Eco.Runtime, and Eco.MVar.

Kernel IO primitives fail with a neutral fixed-layout tuple
`( classificationTag, path, message )` (see IO_ERR_002). The `Raw*` record is
the Elm-facing neutral representation assembled from that tuple; `decodeIOError`
maps it into the typed `IOError` ADT. The classification tag is computed at each
kernel (native C++ maps `errno`; JS/XHR map `err.code`) so this single decode
covers all backends.

@docs IOError, RawIOError
@docs fromKernel, decodeIOError, ofKernelTuple
@docs toString

unions.name: IOError
unions.comment:
A structured IO error. Each constructor corresponds to a stable
classification tag emitted by the kernels.
cases: { tag : Basics.Int, path : Maybe.Maybe String.String, message : String.String }

aliases.name: RawIOError
aliases.comment: The neutral record assembled from the kernel failure tuple.
aliases.type: { tag : Basics.Int, path : String.String, message : String.String }

values.name: decodeIOError
values.comment:
Map the neutral record into the typed `IOError`. The tag values are the
stable contract shared with the kernels (see IO_ERR_002).

values.name: fromKernel
values.comment:
Assemble the neutral record from a kernel failure tuple
`( classificationTag, path, message )`.

values.name: ofKernelTuple
values.comment: Convenience: decode straight from the kernel failure tuple.

values.name: tagFromCode
values.comment:
Map a Node/libuv-style errno code string (e.g. "ENOENT") to the stable
classification tag. Used by the XHR path, where the eco-io server forwards the
error `code` string rather than a numeric errno. Keep in sync with the C++ and
JS kernel errno classification (see IO_ERR_002).

values.name: toString
values.comment: A short human-readable description, for embedding in larger messages.

name: Eco.MVar
comment:
MVar concurrency primitives: create, read, take, and put.

MVars are mutable variables that can be empty or full. Operations on empty
or full MVars block until the MVar reaches the required state.

The kernel implementation stores Elm values directly in a JS-side dict
without any Bytes serialization. The encoder/decoder parameters are accepted
for API compatibility with the XHR variant but are ignored at runtime.


# Types

@docs MVar


# Operations

@docs new, read, take, put, drop

unions.name: MVar
unions.comment:
An opaque mutable variable that can hold a value of type `a`.
An MVar is either empty or contains exactly one value.

values.name: drop
values.comment:
Destroy an MVar, removing it from the store entirely.
Any pending waiters are abandoned. Use only when no further access will occur.

values.name: new
values.comment: Create a new empty MVar.

values.name: put
values.comment:
Put a value into an MVar. Blocks if the MVar is already full.
The encoder parameter is ignored; the value is stored directly in the
JS-side store without serialization.
values.type: (a -> Bytes.Encode.Encoder) -> Eco.MVar.MVar a -> a -> Task.Task Basics.Never ()

values.name: read
values.comment:
Read the value from an MVar without removing it.
Blocks if the MVar is empty. The decoder parameter is ignored; values are
returned directly from the JS-side store.
values.type: Bytes.Decode.Decoder a -> Eco.MVar.MVar a -> Task.Task Basics.Never a

values.name: take
values.comment:
Take the value from an MVar, leaving it empty.
Blocks if the MVar is empty. The decoder parameter is ignored; values are
returned directly from the JS-side store.
values.type: Bytes.Decode.Decoder a -> Eco.MVar.MVar a -> Task.Task Basics.Never a

name: Eco.NativeDriver
comment:
In-process MLIR lowering and native linking.

These functions are only meaningful when called from the unified `eco`
binary, which statically links the `EcoNativeDriverStatic` library that
provides the actual MLIR → ELF pipeline. Other front-end builds (Stage 1
guida.js, Stages 2–4 eco-boot*.js, eco-compiler) link only a stub that
returns a Task failure if these are invoked. The bootstrap stages never
reach the call site, so the stubs stay unexercised.


# Lowering

@docs lowerAndLink, lowerAndLinkBytes

values.name: lowerAndLink
values.comment:
Lower the MLIR text at `mlirPath` to an ELF executable at `outputPath`.

`rootModule` is the program's root module name (e.g. "Top"). It is baked
into the output as the `__eco_root_module` symbol so the N-API addon
exposes the app at `Elm.<RootModule>` — the same path host JS uses with
the JS target. Pass "" to omit the symbol (the addon falls back to "Main").

Runs the full pipeline in-process: parse MLIR, run the Eco → LLVM pass
pipeline, translate to LLVM IR, run RS4GC + opt + object emission, then
link via the system `ld` with the runtime and kernel static libraries
baked into the binary.

Fails with a `String` error message if any pipeline stage returns
nonzero. The most common failure is the "native driver unavailable"
case (rc=-1) — returned when this binary doesn't link
EcoNativeDriverStatic and only the weak stub of
`eco_native_lower_and_link` is present (e.g. eco-compiler, or any
AOT-compiled user program produced by `eco`).

values.name: lowerAndLinkBytes
values.comment:
In-memory MLIR variant: lower MLIR text bytes directly to an ELF at
`outputPath` without a temp `.mlir` file on disk. Used by Phase 2 of the
single-binary plan.

Same failure semantics as `lowerAndLink` — fails with a `String` error
message on any pipeline-stage failure.

name: Eco.Process

unions.name: ExitCode
unions.comment: The exit code of a completed process.

unions.name: ProcessHandle
unions.comment: An opaque handle to a running external process.

unions.name: StdStream
unions.comment: How to handle a standard stream when spawning a process.

values.name: exit
values.comment: Exit the current process with the given exit code. Never returns.

values.name: spawn
values.comment: Spawn an external process with inherited stdio. Returns a process handle.
values.type: String.String -> List.List String.String -> Task.Task Eco.Process.Error.ProcessError Eco.Process.ProcessHandle

values.name: spawnProcess
values.comment:
Spawn an external process with configurable stdio.
Returns a record with stdinHandle (optionally a stdin handle ID if stdin was CreatePipe)
and processHandle. The stdin handle ID can be used with Console.write and File.close.
values.type: { cmd : String.String, args : List.List String.String, stdin : Eco.Process.StdStream, stdout : Eco.Process.StdStream, stderr : Eco.Process.StdStream } -> Task.Task Eco.Process.Error.ProcessError { stdinHandle : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, processHandle : Eco.Process.ProcessHandle }

values.name: wait
values.comment: Wait for a process to complete and return its exit code.

name: Eco.Process.Error

values.name: decodeProcessError
values.comment: Map the neutral IO error record into a `ProcessError`, given the command.

values.name: ofKernelTuple
values.comment:
Convenience: decode straight from the kernel failure tuple, given the
command being spawned.

values.name: toString
values.comment: A short human-readable description, for embedding in larger messages.

name: Eco.Runtime
comment:
Runtime-specific operations: script directory, random numbers, REPL state.

All operations are atomic IO primitives backed by kernel implementations.


# Operations

@docs dirname, random, saveState, loadState

values.name: dirname
values.comment: Get the directory of the current script or binary.

values.name: loadState
values.comment: Load the REPL state from runtime storage.

values.name: random
values.comment: Get a random Float between 0 (inclusive) and 1 (exclusive).

values.name: saveState
values.comment: Persist the REPL state to runtime storage.
